---
title: "Jiale Zhao"
page-layout: full
image: image.png
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: solana
  image-width: 12em
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: mailbox
      text: jialeuuz@gmail.com
      href: mailto:jialeuuz@gmail.com
    - icon: github
      text: jialeuuz
      href: https://github.com/jialeuuz
    - icon: journal-text
      text: OpenReview
      href: https://openreview.net/profile?id=~Jiale_Zhao5
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/jiale-zhao-038662309
    - icon: person-vcard
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0008-4229-1690
    - icon: file-earmark-pdf
      text: CV
      href: cv.pdf
---

::: {#hero-heading}

Hi! I'm Jiale Zhao, a computer science graduate (B.Eng., 2025) from Chongqing University of Posts and Telecommunications. I currently work on rubric‑based RLVR for large language models during my internship at Li Auto.

I plan to begin a PhD in Fall 2026. My interests center on large language models and applied NLP, including:

- Human-centered human–AI interaction (HCI)
- Agent-based LLMs and multi-step reasoning
- Rubric-based RLVR
- Self-evolving systems
- Interpretability and controllability
- End-to-end multimodal interactive systems (e.g., GPT-4o)

## Background

- Fall 2026 (planned): PhD studies (applications in progress)
- Sep 2021 – Jun 2025: B.Eng., Computer Science, Chongqing University of Posts and Telecommunications

## Selected Publications

::: {.pub-note}
For full links and the complete publication list, see the
[Publications](publications.qmd) page.
:::

### Accepted

::: {.pub-list .pub-list-compact}
::: {.pub-entry .pub-entry-compact}
::: {.pub-title}
[When and What to Ask: AskBench and Rubric-Guided RLVR for LLM Clarification](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.11199)
:::

::: {.pub-authors}
**Jiale Zhao**, Ke Fang, Lu Cheng
:::

::: {.pub-meta}
[Findings of ACL 2026]{.pub-badge .pub-badge-accepted} [First author]{.pub-role}
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry .pub-entry-compact}
::: {.pub-title}
[RubricHub: A Comprehensive and Highly Discriminative Rubric Dataset via Automated Coarse-to-Fine Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2601.08430)
:::

::: {.pub-authors}
Sunzhu Li, **Jiale Zhao**, Miteto Wei, Huimin Ren, Yang Zhou, Jingwen Yang, Shunyu Liu, Kaike Zhang, Wei Chen
:::

::: {.pub-meta}
[ACL 2026 Oral]{.pub-badge .pub-badge-accepted} [Second author]{.pub-role}
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry .pub-entry-compact}
::: {.pub-title}
[ThinkPilot: Steering Reasoning Models via Automated Think-prefixes Optimization](https://aclanthology.org/2026.findings-eacl.185/)
:::

::: {.pub-authors}
Sunzhu Li, Zhiyu Lin, **Jiale Zhao**, Shuling Yang, Chen Wei
:::

::: {.pub-meta}
[Findings of EACL 2026]{.pub-badge .pub-badge-accepted} [Co-author]{.pub-role}
:::
:::
:::

### Under Review

::: {.pub-list .pub-list-compact}
::: {.pub-entry .pub-entry-compact}
::: {.pub-title}
[Breaking the Exploration Bottleneck: Rubric-Scaffolded Reinforcement Learning for General LLM Reasoning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.16949)
:::

::: {.pub-authors}
Yang Zhou, Sunzhu Li, Shunyu Liu, Wenkai Fang, Kongcheng Zhang, **Jiale Zhao**, Jingwen Yang, Yihe Zhou, Jianwei Lv, Tongya Zheng, Hengtong Lu, Chen Wei, Xie Yan, Mingli Song
:::

::: {.pub-meta}
[ICML 2026 Submission]{.pub-badge .pub-badge-review} [Sixth author]{.pub-role}
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry .pub-entry-compact}
::: {.pub-title}
[Decoding the Ear: A Framework for Objectifying Expressiveness from Human Preference Through Efficient Alignment](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.20513)
:::

::: {.pub-authors}
Zhiyu Lin, Jingwen Yang, **Jiale Zhao**, Meng Liu, Sunzhu Li, Benyou Wang
:::

::: {.pub-meta}
[Interspeech 2026 Submission]{.pub-badge .pub-badge-review} [Third author]{.pub-role}
:::
:::
:::

## Ongoing Work

- **ProcessRubrics** — second-author collaboration with Ke Fang and
  Prof. Lu Cheng (UIC) on process-level rubric learning for improving
  structured reasoning quality.
- **Learning Persona as Behavior** — first-author collaboration
  with Prof. Lu Cheng (UIC) on behavior-oriented persona learning
  with stronger cross-domain stability.

## Selected Projects
All three were production business deliverables I shipped during my Li Auto internship.

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
#### Data Flywheel for Code LLM
Evaluation-centric loop (SFT → evaluate → data build → filtering → back to SFT) to continually raise coding capabilities.

- *Evaluation-first:* Code-eval today is noisy—difficulty too low, specs ambiguous—so I standardized harnesses and rubrics to push harder tasks and capture real capability.
- *Linked loops:* Evaluation feedback drives harder data construction; the same tooling filters low-quality samples; filtered data re-enters the generation stack for repair and resurfacing.
:::

::: {.project-card}
#### Multi-step Reasoning + Tool Invocation Agent
Code-LLM agent that plans, writes code, and executes tool calls for precise answers.

- *Multi-step reasoning:* Breaks complex or code-debugging tasks into structured plans so context can be stitched into a single executable query.
- *Tool grounding:* Integrates function calls/code execution for real-time data, external APIs, and environment actions when model priors or knowledge bases fall short.
:::

::: {.project-card}
#### MindGPTo (GPT‑4o-style multimodal app)
End-to-end audio + vision application with paralinguistic control, built from scratch with a modular FE/BE split.

- *Mode coverage:* Ships traditional audio→ASR→LLM→TTS, production audio2text→TTS pipelines, end-to-end audio2audio, and multimodal audio+image+video→text→TTS workflows.
- *Paralinguistic SFT:* Large-scale audio data pipelines boost colloquial speech and nuanced cues (beyond laughter/pauses) such as age, gender, compound emotions, emotional actions, and ambient sounds.
:::

:::

:::
